summary: Broad item-subsystem decomp progress touching 25 files. The PR moved itbombhei.c to Matching in configure.py, adjusted item symbol metadata across data/rodata/sdata/sdata2, added or refined item structs/prototypes, and made many small source-shape changes that the bot report says produced 32 new matches and 34 unmatched-item improvements.

evidence_refs: text_corpus:bot report lists itbombhei .data at 100% and several itbombhei function matches

what_changed: PR body reported remaining item TUs/functions as 20 TUs and 90 functions.
Bot report recorded +0.38% matched code (+14840 bytes), +0.39% linked code (+15092 bytes), +0.07% matched data (+864 bytes), and +0.03% linked data (+328 bytes).
Bot report recorded 32 new matches and 34 improvements in unmatched items.
configure.py changed itbombhei.c from NonMatching to Matching.
symbols.txt adjusted item-related data metadata, including localizing many string/float symbols and changing some sizes/splits.
itCharItems.h introduced AnimBundle and used it in itLinkBoomerangAttributes.
it_2725.h and it_2725.c changed fn_80277D8C from a two-argument to a three-argument function signature and cast it when assigning to HSD_MObjSetupFunc.
Several item and item-core C files changed local declaration order, temporaries, pointer caching, control-flow shape, volatile/PAD_STACK usage, and typed helper structs.

smart_moves.text: Updated the owning header when a function signature changed, instead of only changing the C definition.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt:it_2725.h prototype gains third u32 parameter

smart_moves.text: Used a small named struct, AnimBundle, to express a repeated anim/matanim/shapeanim layout in item attributes.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt:itCharItems.h added AnimBundle and applied it to x4C_anim/x58_anim

smart_moves.text: Preserved data ownership detail by changing symbol metadata scope/name/size instead of only compensating in C source.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt:config/GALE01/symbols.txt local @ labels and adjusted sizes/splits

smart_moves.text: For match-sensitive code, introduced narrow source-shape tactics such as cached globals, pointer-to-field temporaries, volatile argument, and PAD_STACK in specific functions rather than broad file-level changes.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt:it_2725.c cached Item_804A0E24 fields in it_8027AB64
diff_excerpt:it_2725.c volatile arg1 and PAD_STACK(4) in it_8027B288

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a recovered callback needs a more exact signature for matching, update both the implementation and its header prototype, and cast at the function-pointer assignment if the target callback typedef differs.
Temporary typed structs can improve item collision/data access readability and matching when raw layouts are being recovered.
For item data progress, symbol metadata changes may include making literals local, correcting sizes, and splitting mixed string/float regions when the diff supports that ownership.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt:global string symbols changed to local @ labels
diff_excerpt:it_803F6A78 split into lbl_803F6A78 string and it_803F6A84 float data
diff_excerpt:.sdata2 globals changed to local float/double labels

decomp_lessons.lesson: Small source-order and local-lifetime changes can close final few-byte item matches; keep the tactic narrow and tied to the specific function.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt:it_2725.c stack-relative color pointer and volatile/PAD_STACK changes
text_corpus:bot report lists many +1 to +20 byte new matches

naming_conventions.name: AnimBundle
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt:itCharItems.h typedef struct { HSD_AnimJoint* anim; HSD_MatAnimJoint* matanim; HSD_ShapeAnimJoint* shapeanim; } AnimBundle
naming_conventions.note: Name reflects the three animation-resource pointers shown in the struct.

naming_conventions.name: ItCollDynamicsDesc / ItCollDynamics
naming_conventions.note: Names describe collision dynamics descriptors and container layout.

naming_conventions.name: recent_x_val, recent_y_val, recent_y
naming_conventions.note: Locals describe cached recent Pokemon selection fields without renaming the underlying globals.

naming_conventions.name: @NNN local labels
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt:symbols.txt multiple it_ address globals replaced by @206, @231, @232, @260, @278, @285, @1588, @1589 local labels
naming_conventions.note: Used for local data symbols in symbols.txt; do not infer semantic names from this PR alone.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Promote an object to Matching only after associated code/data report shows completion.
evidence_refs: text_corpus:bot report itbombhei .data 100% and multiple itbombhei functions 100%

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use pointer-to-field temporaries to influence load/store shape for table allocation/fill code.
Rewrite chained boolean expression as sequential OR-assignment when evaluation/order/register shape matters.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt:it_266F.c it_8026D3CC changed from single return expression to result |= sequence

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use cached global values and a local pointer to preserve exact global access/update order in selection loops.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt:it_2725.c it_8027AB64 caches Item_804A0E24.x/y and uses recent_y pointer

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use a labeled exit/goto selected for a loop selection path when break/fallthrough source shape does not match.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt:it_2725.c break replaced by goto selected and selected label

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use volatile parameter and PAD_STACK narrowly for a one-byte near-match.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt:it_2725.c it_8027B288 volatile u32 arg1 and PAD_STACK(4)

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Represent stack aliasing explicitly with a stack-relative pointer cast when a local object must occupy a particular slot.

notes: Changed-file metadata is complete for 25 files, but the loaded diff excerpt is truncated, so exact edits for several item files are not visible.
The bot report provides matching-progress evidence, but no local build/objdiff command output from the author is loaded.
Review has an approval state but no substantive review comments; reviewer intent beyond approval is not inferred.
Some source-shape tactics are visible in the diff, but the PR does not explicitly explain why each tactic was needed.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Header prototype should track recovered function signature
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR2533 changed fn_80277D8C in both it_2725.c and it_2725.h when adding a third u32 parameter, and cast the function at the HSD_MObjSetupFunc assignment site.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Correct symbol metadata rather than hiding data ownership in C
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR2533 made many item data symbols local, adjusted sizes, and split a data region into separate string/float symbols in symbols.txt while making source progress.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt:symbols.txt local @ labels and size/split changes

accepted_candidate_records.title: Narrow stack/register tactics can finish near-matches but need verifier context
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR2533 used narrow match-shaping tactics such as volatile arg1, PAD_STACK(4), stack-relative pointer casts, cached globals, and pointer-to-field temporaries in specific functions that the bot report lists as new matches or improvements.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt:it_2725.c volatile/PAD_STACK and stack-relative GXColor pointer

source_update_candidates.title: Item decomp path coverage for PR2533
source_update_candidates.text: PR2533 touched broad item subsystem paths: item core files under src/melee/it, many individual item files under src/melee/it/items, item headers, configure.py, and config/GALE01/symbols.txt.
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful for path-scope search and routing future item decomp PRs; curator should decide whether this is source-owned path metadata.

source_update_candidates.title: Callback signature recovery with assignment-site casts
source_update_candidates.text: When a recovered function body indicates a more specific callback signature than the callback typedef, update the owning header prototype and use an explicit cast at the assignment site rather than leaving the header stale.
source_update_candidates.reason: Consistent with truthful-headers standard, but this PR is only one example; curator should decide if the cast-specific guidance belongs globally.

source_update_candidates.title: Treat volatile/PAD_STACK and stack-relative aliasing as narrow final-match tactics
source_update_candidates.text: PR2533 contains narrow uses of volatile parameters, PAD_STACK, and stack-relative pointer casts in functions reported as near-final matches; these should remain verifier-backed tactics rather than general style.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt:it_8027B288 volatile u32 arg1 and PAD_STACK(4)
text_corpus:bot report +1 byte matches for it_80278108 and it_8027B288
source_update_candidates.reason: Already aligns with matching-tactics-need-evidence; curator may add PR2533 as an example rather than a new standard.

source_update_candidates.title: AnimBundle layout candidate in item character attributes
source_update_candidates.text: PR2533 added AnimBundle as three pointers: HSD_AnimJoint* anim, HSD_MatAnimJoint* matanim, and HSD_ShapeAnimJoint* shapeanim, then used it for itLinkBoomerangAttributes x4C_anim and x58_anim.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt:itCharItems.h AnimBundle typedef and field replacements
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential reusable data-layout fact; should be curated against current headers and any later renames before promotion.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that all changed item files became matching; only itbombhei.c is explicitly promoted in configure.py in the loaded diff.
Do not infer detailed semantic names or behavior for item functions from bot match names alone.
Do not treat every symbols.txt local-label change as a universal rule; these are PR-local metadata edits unless corroborated by split/section ownership evidence.
Do not claim reviewer rationale; loaded review data only shows approval with no body.
